text.Base: The book of the acts of Tobit son of Tobiel son of Hananiel son of Aduel son of Gabael son of Raphael son of Raguel, of the descendants of Asiel, of the tribe of Naphtali,
text.KJV: 1:1 The Booke of the wordes of Tobit, sonne of Tobiel, the son of Ananiel, the sonne of Aduel, the sonne of Gabael, of the seed of Asael, of the Tribe of Nephthali,
text.DRV: 1:1 Tobias of the tribe and city of Nephthali (which is in the upper parts of Galilee above Naasson, beyond the way that leadeth to the west, having on the right hand the city Sephet)

text.Base: who in the days of King Shalmaneser of the Assyrians was taken into captivity from Thisbe, which is to the south of Kedesh Naphtali in Upper Galilee, above Asher toward the west, and north of Phogor.
text.KJV: 1:2 Who in the time of Enemessar king of the Assyrians, was led captiue out of This be which is at the right hand of that citie, which is called properly Nephthali in Galile aboue Aser.
text.DRV: 1:2 when he was captive in the days of Salmanasar the king of the Assyrians, yet being in captivity, he forsook not the way of truth,

text.Base: I, Tobit, walked in the ways of truth and righteousness all the days of my life. I performed many acts of charity for my kindred and my people who had gone with me in exile to Nineveh in the land of the Assyrians.
text.KJV: 1:3 I Tobit haue walked all the dayes of my life in the way of trueth, and iustice, and I did many almes deeds to my brethren, and my nation, who came with me to Nineue into the land of the Assyrians.
text.DRV: 1:3 so that he imparted all things that he could make daily to his brethren captives with him, which were of his kindred.

text.Base: When I was in my own country, in the land of Israel, while I was still a young man, the whole tribe of my ancestor Naphtali deserted the house of David and Jerusalem. This city had been chosen from among all the tribes of Israel, where all the tribes of Israel should offer sacrifice and where the temple, the dwelling of God, had been consecrated and established for all generations forever.
text.KJV: 1:4 And when I was in mine owne countrey, in the land of Israel, being but yong, all the tribe of Nephthali my father, fell from the house of Ierusalem, which was chosen out of all the tribes of Israel, that all the tribes should sacrifice there where the Temple of the habitation of the most High was consecrated, and built for all ages.
text.DRV: 1:4 And whereas he was younger than all the tribe of Nephthali, yet did he no childish thing in his work.

text.Base: All my kindred and our ancestral house used to sacrifice to the calf that King Jeroboam of Israel had erected in Dan and on all the mountains of Galilee.
text.KJV: 1:5 Now all the tribes which together reuolted, and the house of my father Nephthali sacrificed vnto the heifer Baal.
text.DRV: 1:5 Finally, when all went to the golden calves, which Jeroboam the king of Israel had made, he alone fled the companies of all,

text.Base: But I alone used to go often to Jerusalem for the festivals, as was prescribed by an everlasting decree for all Israel. I would hurry off to Jerusalem with the first fruits of the crops and the firstlings of the flock, the tithes of the cattle, and the first shearings of the sheep.
text.KJV: 1:6 But I alone went often to Ierusalem at the Feasts, as it was ordeined vnto al the people of Israel by an euerlasting decree, hauing the first fruits, and tenths of encrease, with that which was first shorne, and them gaue I at the Altar to the Priestes the children of Aaron.
text.DRV: 1:6 and went into Jerusalem to the temple of our Lord, and there adored our Lord God of Israel, offering faithfully all his first fruits, and his tithes,

text.Base: I would give these to the priests, the sons of Aaron, at the altar; likewise the tenth of the grain, wine, olive oil, pomegranates, figs, and other fruits to the sons of Levi who ministered at Jerusalem. Also for six years I would save up a second tenth in money and go and distribute it in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 1:7 The first tenth part of al increase, I gaue to the sonnes of Aaron, who ministred at Ierusalem: another tenth part I sold away, and went, and spent it euery yeere at Ierusalem.
text.DRV: 1:7 so that in the third year he ministered all the tithing to the proselytes and strangers.

text.Base: A third tenth I would give to the orphans and widows and to the converts who had attached themselves to Israel. I would bring it and give it to them in the third year, and we would eat it according to the ordinance decreed concerning it in the law of Moses and according to the instructions of Deborah, the mother of my father Tobiel, for my father had died and left me an orphan.
text.KJV: 1:8 And the third, I gaue vnto them to whom it was meet, as Debora my fathers mother had commanded mee, because I was left an orphane by my father.
text.DRV: 1:8 These things and the like to these did he observe being a child according to the law of God.

text.Base: When I became a man I married Anna, a member of our own family, and by her I became the father of Tobias.
text.KJV: 1:9 Furthermore when I was come to the age of a man, I married Anna of mine owne kinred, and of her I begate Tobias.
text.DRV: 1:9 But when he was a man, he took to wife Anna of his own tribe, and he begat a son of her, giving him his own name.

text.Base: After I was carried away captive to Assyria and came to Nineveh, everyone of my kindred and my people ate the food of the Gentiles,
text.KJV: 1:10 And when we were caried away captiues to Nineue, all my brethren, and those that were of my kinred, did eate of the bread of the Gentiles.
text.DRV: 1:10 Whom from his infancy he taught to fear God, and to abstain from all sin.

text.Base: but I kept myself from eating the food of the Gentiles.
text.KJV: 1:11 But I kept my selfe from eating
text.DRV: 1:11 Therefore when by the captivity he was come with his wife and son into the city of Ninive, with all his tribe,

text.Base: Because I was mindful of God with all my heart,
text.KJV: 1:12 Because I remembred God with all my heart.
text.DRV: 1:12 (When all did eat of the meats of the Gentiles) he kept his soul, and never was contaminated in their meats.

text.Base: the Most High gave me favor and good standing with Shalmaneser, and I used to buy everything he needed.
text.KJV: 1:13 And the most High gaue me grace, and fauour before Enemessar, so that I was his purueyour.
text.DRV: 1:13 And because he was mindful of our Lord in all his heart, God gave him grace in the sight of Salmanasar the king,

text.Base: Until his death I used to go into Media, and buy for him there. At Gabael’s house in Rhages of Media I left bags of silver in trust, ten talents’ weight.
text.KJV: 1:14 And I went into Media, and left in trust with Gabael, the brother of Gabrias at Rages a citie of Media, ten talents of siluer.
text.DRV: 1:14 and he gave him leave to go whithersoever he would, having liberty to do what things soever he would.

text.Base: When Shalmaneser died, and his son Sennacherib reigned in his place, the highways into Media became unsafe and I could no longer go there.
text.KJV: 1:15 Now when Enemessar was dead, Sennacherib his sonne reigned in his stead, whose estate was troubled, that I could not goe into Media.
text.DRV: 1:15 He therefore went to all that were in the captivity, and gave them wholesome admonitions.

text.Base: In the days of Shalmaneser I performed many acts of charity to my kindred, those of my people.
text.KJV: 1:16 And in the time of Enemessar, I gaue many almes to my brethren, and gaue my bread to the hungry,
text.DRV: 1:16 And when he was come into Rages a city of the Medes, and had ten talents of silver of these wherewith he had been honoured of the king:

text.Base: I would give my food to the hungry and my clothing to the naked; and if I saw the dead body of any of my people thrown out behind the wall of Nineveh, I would bury it.
text.KJV: 1:17 And my clothes to the naked: and if I saw any of my nation dead, or cast about the walles of Nineue, I buried him.
text.DRV: 1:17 and when in a great multitude of his kindred, he saw Gabelus stand in need, who was of his tribe, under a bill of his hand he gave him the said weight of silver.

text.Base: I also buried any whom Sennacherib put to death when he came fleeing from Judea in those days of judgment that the King of heaven executed upon him because of his blasphemies. For in his anger he put to death many of the Israelites; but I would secretly remove the bodies and bury them. So when Sennacherib looked for them he could not find them.
text.KJV: 1:18 And if the king Sennacherib had slaine any, when hee was come, and fledde from Iudea, I buried them priuily, (for in his wrath hee killed many) but the bodies were not found, when they were sought for of the king.
text.DRV: 1:18 But after much time, Salmanasar the king being dead, when Sennacherib his son reigned for him, & esteemed the children of Israel odious in his sight:

text.Base: Then one of the Ninevites went and informed the king about me, that I was burying them; so I hid myself. But when I realized that the king knew about me and that I was being searched for to be put to death, I was afraid and ran away.
text.KJV: 1:19 And when one of the Nineuites went, and complained of me to the king that I buried them, and hid my selfe: vnderstanding that I was sought for to be put to death, I withdrew my selfe for feare.
text.DRV: 1:19 Tobias daily went through all his kindred, and comforted them, and divided to every one, as he was able, of his goods:

text.Base: Then all my property was confiscated; nothing was left to me that was not taken into the royal treasury except my wife Anna and my son Tobias.
text.KJV: 1:20 Then all my goods were forcibly taken away, neither was there any thing left me, besides my wife Anna, and my sonne Tobias.
text.DRV: 1:20 the hungry he nourished, and to the naked he gave cloths, and the dead, and them that were slain, he buried carefully.

text.Base: But not forty days passed before two of Sennacherib’s sons killed him, and they fled to the mountains of Ararat, and his son Esar-haddon reigned after him. He appointed Ahikar, son of my brother Hanael, over all the accounts of his kingdom, and he had authority over the entire administration.
text.KJV: 1:21 And there passed not fiue and fiftie dayes before two of his sonnes killed him, and they fled into the mountaines of Ararath, and Sarchedonus his sonne reigned in his stead, who appointed ouer his fathers accounts, and ouer all his affaires, Achiacharus my brother Anaels sonne.
text.DRV: 1:21 Finally when king Sennacherib was returned flying from Jewry the slaughter, that God had made about him for his blasphemy, & being angry slew many of the children of Israel, Tobias buried their bodies.

text.Base: Ahikar interceded for me, and I returned to Nineveh. Now Ahikar was chief cupbearer, keeper of the signet, and in charge of administration of the accounts under King Sennacherib of Assyria; so Esar-haddon reappointed him. He was my nephew and so a member of my family.
text.KJV: 1:22 And Achiacharus entreating for me, I returned to Nineue: now Achiacharus was Cup-bearer, and keeper of the Signet, and Steward, and ouerseer of the accounts: and Sarchedonus appointed him next vnto him: and hee was my brothers sonne.
text.DRV: 1:22 But when it was told the king, he commanded him to be slain, and took all his substance.

text.Base: Then during the reign of Esar-haddon I returned home, and my wife Anna and my son Tobias were restored to me. At our festival of Pentecost, which is the sacred festival of seven weeks, a good dinner was prepared for me and I reclined to eat.
text.KJV: 2:1 Now when I was come home againe, and my wife Anna was restored vnto me, with my sonne Tobias, in the feast of Pentecost, which is the holy Feast of the seuen weekes, there was a good dinner prepared me, in the which I sate down to eate.
text.DRV: 2:1 But after these things, when there was a festival day of our Lord, and a good dinner was made in Tobias' house,

text.Base: When the table was set for me and an abundance of food placed before me, I said to my son Tobias, “Go, my child, and see if you can find some poor person of our kindred among the exiles in Nineveh who is mindful of God, and bring him to join me in eating the dinner. I will wait for you, my child, until you come back.”
text.KJV: 2:2 And when I saw abundance of meate, I sayd to my sonne, Goe and bring what poore man soeuer thou shalt finde out of our brethren, who is mindfull of the Lord, and loe, I tarie for thee.
text.DRV: 2:2 he said to his son: Go, and bring some of our tribe that fear God, to make merry with us.

text.Base: So Tobias went to look for some poor person of our kindred. When he had returned he said, “Father!” And I replied, “Here I am, my child.” Then he went on to say, “Look, father, one of our own people has been murdered and thrown into the market place, and now he lies there strangled.”
text.KJV: 2:3 But he came againe and said, Father, one of our nation is strangled, and is cast out in the market place.
text.DRV: 2:3 And when he had gone, returning he told him, that one of the children of Israel lay slain in the street. And he forthwith leaping up from his place at the table, leaving his dinner, came fasting to the body:

text.Base: Then I sprang up, left the dinner before even tasting it, and removed the body from the square and laid it in one of the rooms until sunset when I might bury it.
text.KJV: 2:4 Then before I had tasted of any meate, I start vp and tooke him vp into a roume, vntill the going downe of the Sunne.
text.DRV: 2:4 and taking it up carried it to his house secretly, that when the sun should be down, he might warily bury him.

text.Base: When I returned, I washed myself and ate my food in sorrow.
text.KJV: 2:5 Then I returned and washed my selfe, and ate my meate in heauinesse,
text.DRV: 2:5 And when he had hid the body, he eat bread with mourning & trembling.

text.Base: Then I remembered the prophecy of Amos, how he said against Bethel, “Your festivals shall be turned into mourning, and all your songs into lamentation.” And I wept.
text.KJV: 2:6 Remembring that prophesie of Amos, as hee said; Your feasts shall be turned into mourning, and all your mirth into lamentation.
text.DRV: 2:6 remembering that word which our Lord said by Amos the Prophet: Your festival days shall be turned into lamentation & mourning.

text.Base: When the sun had set, I went and dug a grave and buried him.
text.KJV: 2:7 Therefore I wept: and after the going downe of the Sunne, I went and made a graue, and buried him.
text.DRV: 2:7 But when the sun was down, he went and buried him.

text.Base: And my neighbors laughed and said, “Is he still not afraid? He has already been hunted down to be put to death for doing this, and he ran away; yet here he is again burying the dead!”
text.KJV: 2:8 But my neighbours mocked me, and said, This man is not yet afraide to be put to death for this matter, who fledde away, and yet loe, he burieth the dead againe.
text.DRV: 2:8 And all his neighbours rebuked him, saying: Even now thou wast commanded to be slain because of this matter, and thou didst scarce escape the commandment of death, and doest thou bury the dead again?

text.Base: That same night I washed myself and went into my courtyard and slept by the wall of the courtyard; and my face was uncovered because of the heat.
text.KJV: 2:9 The same night also I returned from the buriall, and slept by the wall of my court yard, being polluted, and my face was vncouered:
text.DRV: 2:9 But Tobias more fearing God than the king, in haste took the bodies of them that were slain, and hid them in his house, & at midnight buried them.

text.Base: I did not know that there were sparrows on the wall; their fresh droppings fell into my eyes and produced white films. I went to physicians to be healed, but the more they treated me with ointments the more my vision was obscured by the white films, until I became completely blind. At that time Ahikar took care of me for two years, until he went to Elymais.
text.KJV: 2:10 And I knewe not that there were Sparrowes in the wall, and mine eyes being open, the Sparrowes muted warme doung into mine eyes, and a whitenesse came in mine eyes, and I went to the Physicians, but they helped me not: moreouer Achiacharus did nourish mee, vntill I went into Elymais.
text.DRV: 2:10 And it happened that on a certain day, being wearied with burying, coming into his house he had cast himself down by the wall, and slept,

text.Base: Then my wife Anna earned money at women’s work.
text.KJV: 2:11 And my wife Anna did take womens workes to doe.
text.DRV: 2:11 and as he was sleeping, hot dung out of the swallows' nest fell upon his eyes, and he was made blind.

text.Base: She used to send the completed work to the owners and they would pay her wages. One day, the seventh of Dystrus, when she cut off her finished work and sent it to the owners, they paid her full wages and also gave her a young goat to take home.
text.KJV: 2:12 And when shee had sent them home to the owners, they payd her wages, and gaue her also besides a kid.
text.DRV: 2:12 And this tentation therefore our Lord permitted to chance unto him, that an example might be given to posterity of his patience, as also of holy Job.

text.Base: When she came to me the goat began to bleat. So I called her and said, “Where did you get this goat? It is surely not stolen, is it? Return it to the owners; for we have no right to eat anything stolen.”
text.KJV: 2:13 And when it was in mine house, and beganne to crie, I said vnto her, From whence is this kidde? is it not stollen? render it to the owners, for it is not lawfull to eate any thing that is stollen.
text.DRV: 2:13 For whereas he feared God always from his infancy, and kept his commandments, he grudged not against God for that the plague of blindness had chanced to him,

text.Base: But she said to me, “It was given to me as a gift in addition to my wages.” But I did not believe her, and told her to return it to the owners; I became flushed with anger against her. Then she replied to me, “Where are your acts of charity? Where are your righteous deeds? These things are known about you!”
text.KJV: 2:14 But shee replyed vpon me, It was giuen for a gift more then the wages: Howbeit I did not beleeue her, but bade her render it to the owners: and I was abashed at her. But she replyed vpon me, Where are thine almes, and thy righteous deedes? behold, thou and all thy workes are knowen.
text.DRV: 2:14 but continued immovable in the fear of God, giving thanks to God all the days of his life.

text.Base: Then in my grief I wept, and I prayed in anguish, saying:
text.KJV: 3:1 Then I being grieued, did weepe, and in my sorrowe prayed, saying,
text.DRV: 3:1 Then Tobias lamented, and began to pray with tears,

text.Base: “You are righteous, O Lord, and all your deeds are just; all your ways are mercy and truth; you judge the whole world.”
text.KJV: 3:2 O Lord, thou art iust and all thy workes, and all thy wayes are mercie and trueth, and thou iudgest truely & iustly for euer.
text.DRV: 3:2 saying: Thou art just o Lord, and all thy judgments are just, & all thy ways mercy, & truth & judgment.

text.Base: “And now, O Lord, remember me and look favorably upon me. Do not punish me for my sins and for my unwitting offenses and those that my ancestors committed before you. They sinned against you,”
text.KJV: 3:3 Remember me, and looke on me, punish me not for my sinnes and ignorances, and the sinnes of my fathers, who haue sinned before thee.
text.DRV: 3:3 And now Lord be mindful of me, and take not revenge of my sins, neither remember the sins of me, or of my parents'.

text.Base: “and disobeyed your commandments. So you gave us over to plunder, exile, and death, to become the talk, the byword, and an object of reproach among all the nations among whom you have scattered us.”
text.KJV: 3:4 For they obeyed not thy commandements, wherefore thou hast deliuered vs for a spoile, and vnto captiuitie, and vnto death, and for a prouerbe of reproch to all the nations among whom we are dispersed.
text.DRV: 3:4 Because we have not obeyed thy commandments, therefore we are delivered in spoil, and captivity, and death, and into a fable, and into reproach to all nations, in which thou hast dispersed us.

text.Base: “And now your many judgments are true in exacting penalty from me for my sins. For we have not kept your commandments and have not walked in accordance with truth before you.”
text.KJV: 3:5 And now thy iudgments are many and true: Deale with me according to my sinnes, and my fathers: because we haue not kept thy commandements, neither haue walked in trueth before thee.
text.DRV: 3:5 And now Lord great are thy judgments, because we have not done according to thy precepts, & have not walked sincerely before thee:

text.Base: “So now deal with me as you will; command my spirit to be taken from me, so that I may be released from the face of the earth and become dust. For it is better for me to die than to live, because I have had to listen to undeserved insults, and great is my sorrow. Lord, command that I be released from my distress; let me go to my eternal abode, and do not, O Lord, turn your face away from me. For it is better for me to die than to see so much distress in my life and to listen to insults.”
text.KJV: 3:6 Now therefore deale with me as seemeth best vnto thee, and command my spirit to be taken from me, that I may be dissolued, and become earth: for it is profitable for me to die, rather then to liue, because I haue heard false reproches, and haue much sorow: command therfore that I may now be deliuered out of this distresse, and goe into the euerlasting place: turne not thy face away from me.
text.DRV: 3:6 and now Lord according to thy will do with me, and command my spirit to be received in peace: for it is expedient for me to die, rather than to live.

text.Base: On the same day, at Ecbatana in Media, it also happened that Sarah daughter of Raguel was insulted by one of her father’s maids.
text.KJV: 3:7 It came to passe the same day, that in Ecbatane a citie of Media, Sara the daughter of Raguel, was also reproched by her fathers maides,
text.DRV: 3:7 The very same day therefore it chanced that Sara the daughter of Raguel in Rages a city of the Medes, she also heard reproach of one of her father's hand-maids,

text.Base: For she had been married to seven husbands, and the wicked demon Asmodeus had killed each of them before they had been with her as is customary for wives. So the maid said to her, “You are the one who kills your husbands! See, you have already been married to seven, and not one of them has borne your name.”
text.KJV: 3:8 Because that she had bin maried to seuen husbands, whom Almodeus the euill spirit had killed, before they had lien with her. Doest thou not knowe, said they, that thou hast strangled thine husbands? thou hast had already seuen husbands, neither wast thou named after any of them.
text.DRV: 3:8 because she had been delivered to seven husbands, and the devil named Asmodeus had killed them forthwith as they were entered in unto her.

text.Base: “Why do you beat us? Because your husbands are dead? Go and join them! May we never see a son or daughter of yours!”
text.KJV: 3:9 Wherefore doest thou beate vs for them? If they be dead, goe thy wayes after them, let vs neuer see of thee either sonne or daughter.
text.DRV: 3:9 Therefore when she rebuked the wench for her fault, she answered her, saying: Let us no more see son of thee, or daughter upon the earth, thou murderer of thy husbands.

text.Base: On that day she was grieved in spirit and wept. She went up to her father’s upper room intending to hang herself. But she thought it over and said, “Never shall they reproach my father, saying to him, ‘You had only one beloved daughter but she hanged herself because of her distress.’ And I shall bring my father in his old age down in sorrow to Hades. It is better for me not to hang myself, but to pray to the Lord that I may die and not have to listen to these insults any longer.”
text.KJV: 3:10 When she heard these things, she was very sorowful, so that she thought to haue strangled her selfe, and she said, I am the onely daughter of my father, and if I doe this, it shall bee a reproch vnto him, and I shall bring his old age with sorow vnto the graue.
text.DRV: 3:10 What, wilt thou kill me also, as thou hast now killed seven husbands? At this voice she went into an higher chamber of her house: and three days and three nights did not eat, nor drink:

text.Base: At that same time, with hands outstretched toward the window, she prayed and said: “Blessed are you, merciful God! Blessed is your name forever; let all your works praise you forever.”
text.KJV: 3:11 Then she prayed toward the window, & said, Blessed art thou, O Lord my God, and thine holy and glorious Name is blessed, and honourable foreuer, let al thy works praise thee for euer.
text.DRV: 3:11 but continuing in prayer with tears besought God, that he would deliver her from this reproach.

text.Base: “And now, Lord, I turn my face to you, and lift up my eyes.”
text.KJV: 3:12 And now, O Lord, I set mine eyes and my face toward thee,
text.DRV: 3:12 And it came to pass the third day, whiles she accomplished her prayer, blessing our Lord,

text.Base: “Command that I be released from the earth and not listen to such insults any more.”
text.KJV: 3:13 And say, take me out of the earth, that I may heare no more the reproch.
text.DRV: 3:13 she said: Blessed is thy name o God of our fathers: who when thou hast been angry, wilt do mercy, and in the time of tribulation forgivest them their sins, that invocate thee.

text.Base: “You know, O Lord, that I am innocent of any defilement with a man,”
text.KJV: 3:14 Thou knowest, Lord, that I am pure from all sinne with man,
text.DRV: 3:14 To thee o Lord I turn my face, to thee I direct mine eyes.

text.Base: “and that I have not disgraced my name or the name of my father in the land of my exile. I am my father’s only child; he has no other child to be his heir; and he has no close relative or young man for whom I should keep myself, to become his wife. Already seven husbands of mine are dead. Why should I still live? But if it is not pleasing to you, O Lord, to take my life, hear me in my disgrace.”
text.KJV: 3:15 And that I neuer polluted my name, nor the name of my father in the land of my captiuitie: I am the onely daughter of my father, neither hath he any child to bee his heire, neither any neere kinseman, nor any sonne of his aliue, to whome I may keepe my selfe for a wife: my seuen husbands are already dead, and why should I liue? but if it please not thee that I should die, command some regard to be had of me, and pitie taken of me, that I heare no more reproch.
text.DRV: 3:15 I desire Lord that thou loose me from the bond of this reproach, or else take me away from the earth.

text.Base: At that very moment, the prayers of both of them were heard in the glorious presence of God.
text.KJV: 3:16 So the prayers of them both were heard before the Maiesty of the great God.
text.DRV: 3:16 Thou knowest Lord that I never coveted a husband, & have kept my soul clean from all concupiscence.

text.Base: So Raphael was sent to heal both of them: Tobit, by removing the white films from his eyes, so that he might see God’s light with his eyes; and Sarah daughter of Raguel, by giving her in marriage to Tobias son of Tobit, and by setting her free from the wicked demon Asmodeus. For Tobias was entitled to have her before all others who had desired to marry her. At that very moment Tobit returned from the courtyard into his house, and Sarah daughter of Raguel came down from her upper room.
text.KJV: 3:17 And Raphael was sent to heale them both, that is, to scale away the whitenesse of Tobits eyes, and to giue Sara the daughter of Raguel, for a wife to Tobias the sonne of Tobit, and to bind Asmodeus the euill spirit, because she belongeth to Tobias by right of inheritance. The selfe same time came Tobit home, and entred into his house, and Sara, the daughter of Raguel came downe from her vpper chamber.
text.DRV: 3:17 Never have I companied myself with sporters: neither have I made myself partaker with them that walk in lightness.

text.Base: That day Tobit remembered the silver that he had left in trust with Gabael at Rhages in Media,
text.KJV: 4:1 In that day Tobit remembred the money, which he had committed to Gabael in Rages of Media,
text.DRV: 4:1 Therefore when Tobias thought his prayer to be heard that he might have died, he called to him Tobias his son,

text.Base: and he said to himself, “Now that I have asked for death, why do I not call my son Tobias and explain to him about this silver before I die?”
text.KJV: 4:2 And said with himselfe, I haue wished for death, wherefore doe I not call for my sonne Tobias, that I may signifie to him of the money before I die.
text.DRV: 4:2 and said to him: Hear my son the words of my mouth, and lay them as a foundation in thy heart.

text.Base: Then he called his son Tobias, and when he came to him he said, “My son, when I die, give me a proper burial. Honor your mother and do not abandon her all the days of her life. Do whatever pleases her, and do not grieve her spirit in any matter.
text.KJV: 4:3 And when he had called him, he said; My sonne, when I am dead, bury me, and despise not thy mother, but honour her all the dayes of thy life, and doe that which shall please her, and greiue her not.
text.DRV: 4:3 When God shall take my soul, bury my body: & thou shalt do honour to thy mother all the days of her life:

text.Base: Remember her, my son, for she faced many dangers for you while you were in her womb. And when she dies, bury her beside me in the same grave.
text.KJV: 4:4 Remember, my sonne, that shee saw many dangers for thee, when thou wast in her wombe, and when shee is dead, bury her by me in one graue.
text.DRV: 4:4 for thou must be mindful what perils, and how great she suffered for thee in her womb.

text.Base: “Revere the Lord all your days, my son, and refuse to sin or to transgress his commandments. Live uprightly all the days of your life, and do not walk in the ways of wrongdoing;
text.KJV: 4:5 My sonne, be mindfull of the Lord our God all thy dayes, and let not thy will be set to sinne, or to transgresse his Commandements: doe vprightly all thy life long, and follow not the wayes of vnrighteousnesse.
text.DRV: 4:5 And when she also shall have accomplished the time of her life, bury her beside me.

text.Base: for those who act in accordance with truth will prosper in all their activities. To all those who practice righteousness
text.KJV: 4:6 For if thou deale truely, thy doings shall prosperously succeed to thee, and to all them that liue iustly.
text.DRV: 4:6 And all the days of thy life have God in thy mind: and beware thou consent not to sin at any time, and pretermit the precepts of our Lord God.

text.Base: give alms from your possessions, and do not let your eye begrudge the gift when you made it. Do not turn your face away from anyone who is poor, and the face of God will not be turned away from you.
text.KJV: 4:7 Giue almes of thy substance, and when thou giuest almes, let not thine eye be enuious, neither turne thy face from any poore, and the face of God shall not be turned away from thee.
text.DRV: 4:7 Of thy substance give alms, & turn not away thy face from any poor person: for so it shall come to pass that neither the face of our Lord shall be turned from thee.

text.Base: If you have many possessions, make your gift from them in proportion; if few, do not be afraid to give according to the little you have.
text.KJV: 4:8 If thou hast abundance, giue almes accordingly: if thou haue but a litle, be not afraid to giue according to that litle.
text.DRV: 4:8 As thou shalt be able, so be merciful.

text.Base: So you will be laying up a good treasure for yourself against the day of necessity.
text.KJV: 4:9 For thou layest vp a good treasure for thy selfe against the day of necessitie.
text.DRV: 4:9 If thou have much, give aboundantly: if thou have little, study to impart also a little willingly.

text.Base: For almsgiving delivers from death and keeps you from going into the Darkness.
text.KJV: 4:10 Because that almes doth deliuer from death, and suffereth not to come into darknesse.
text.DRV: 4:10 For thou doest treasure up to thyself a good reward in the day of necessity.

text.Base: Indeed, almsgiving, for all who practice it, is an excellent offering in the presence of the Most High.
text.KJV: 4:11 For almes is a good gift vnto all that giue it, in the sight of the most High.
text.DRV: 4:11 Because alms delivereth from all sin, and from death, and will not suffer the soul to go into darkness.

text.Base: “Beware, my son, of every kind of fornication. Above all, marry a woman of the lineage of your ancestors. Do not marry a foreign woman, who is not of your father’s tribe; for we are the descendants of the prophets. Remember, my son, that Noah, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, our ancestors of old, all took wives from among their kindred. They were blessed in their children, and their posterity will inherit the land.
text.KJV: 4:12 Beware of all whoredome, my sonne, and chiefely take a wife of the seed of thy fathers, and take not a strange woman to wife, which is not of thy fathers tribe: for we are the children of the Prophets, Noe, Abraham, Isaak, and Iacob: remember, my sonne, that our fathers from the beginning, euen that they all maried wiues of their owne kinred, and were blessed in their children, and their seede shall inherite the land.
text.DRV: 4:12 Great confidence before God most high shall alms be to all them that do it.

text.Base: So now, my son, love your kindred, and in your heart do not disdain the children of your people by refusing to take a wife for yourself from among them. For in pride there is ruin and great confusion. And in shiftlessness there is loss and great want, because shiftlessness is the mother of famine.
text.KJV: 4:13 Now therefore my sonne, loue thy brethren, and despise not in thy heart thy brethren, the sonnes and daughters of thy people, in not taking a wife of them: for in pride is destruction and much trouble, and in lewdnesse is decay, and great want: for lewdnesse is the mother of famine.
text.DRV: 4:13 Take heed to thyself my son of all fornication, and beside thy wife never abide to know crime,

text.Base: “Do not hold over till the next day the wages of any who work for you, but pay them at once; and if you serve God you will receive payment. Watch yourself, my son, in everything you do, and discipline yourself in all your conduct.
text.KJV: 4:14 Let not the wages of any man, which hath wrought for thee, tary with thee, but giue him it out of hand: for if thou serue God he will also repay thee: be circumspect, my sonne, in all things thou doest, and be wise in all thy conuersation.
text.DRV: 4:14 Never permit pride to rule in thy word: for in it all perdition took his beginning.

text.Base: And what you hate, do not do to anyone. Do not drink wine to excess or let drunkenness go with you on your way.
text.KJV: 4:15 Doe that to no man which thou hatest: drinke not wine to make thee drunken; neither let drunkennesse goe with thee in thy iourney.
text.DRV: 4:15 Whosoever hath wrought any thing for thee, pay him his hire immediately, and let not the hire of thy hired servant remain with thee at all.

text.Base: Give some of your food to the hungry, and some of your clothing to the naked. Give all your surplus as alms, and do not let your eye begrudge the gift when you give it.
text.KJV: 4:16 Giue of thy bread to the hungry, and of thy garments to them that are naked, and according to thine abundance giue almes, and let not thine eye be enuious, when thou giuest almes.
text.DRV: 4:16 That which thou hatest to be done to thee by another, see thou do it not to another at any time.

text.Base: Place your bread on the grave of the righteous, but give none to sinners.
text.KJV: 4:17 Powre out thy bread on the buriall of the iust, but giue nothing to the wicked.
text.DRV: 4:17 Eat thy bread with the hungry & needy, and of thy garments cover the naked.

text.Base: Seek advice from every wise person and do not despise any useful counsel.
text.KJV: 4:18 Aske counsell of all that are wise, and despise not any counsell that is profitable.
text.DRV: 4:18 Set thy bread, and thy wine upon the burial of a just man, and do not eat and drink thereof with sinners.

text.Base: At all times bless the Lord God, and ask him that your ways may be made straight and that all your paths and plans may prosper. For none of the nations has understanding, but the Lord gives all good things; and according to his will he humbles whomever he wishes. So, my son, remember these my commandments, and do not let them be erased from your heart.
text.KJV: 4:19 Blesse the Lord thy God alway, and desire of him that thy wayes may be directed, and that all thy pathes, and counsels may prosper: for euery nation hath not counsell, but the Lord himselfe giueth all good things, and hee humbleth whom he will, as he will; now therefore my sonne, remember my commandements, neither let them be put out of thy minde.
text.DRV: 4:19 Seek counsel always of a wise man.

text.Base: “And now, my son, let me explain to you that I left ten talents of silver in trust with Gabael son of Gabrias, at Rhages in Media.
text.KJV: 4:20 And now I signifie this to thee, that I committed tenne talents to Gabael the sonne of Gabrias at Rages in Media.
text.DRV: 4:20 At all time bless God: and desire of him, that he direct thy ways, and that all thy counsels remain in him.

text.Base: Do not be afraid, my son, because we have become poor. You have great wealth if you fear God and flee from every sin and do what is good in the sight of the Lord your God.”
text.KJV: 4:21 And feare not my sonne, that we are made poore, for thou hast much wealth, if thou feare God, and depart from all sinne, and doe that which is pleasing in his sight.
text.DRV: 4:21 I tell thee also my son that I gave ten talents of silver, whiles thou wast yet a child, to Gabelus, in Rages a city of the Medes, & I have a bill of his hand with me:

text.Base: Then Tobias answered his father Tobit, “I will do everything that you have commanded me, father;
text.KJV: 5:1 Tobias then answered and said, Father, I will doe all things, which thou hast commanded me.
text.DRV: 5:1 Then Tobias answered his father, and said: I will do all things, father, whatsoever thou hast commanded me.

text.Base: but how can I obtain the money from him, since he does not know me and I do not know him? What evidence am I to give him so that he will recognize and trust me, and give me the money? Also, I do not know the roads to Media, or how to go there.”
text.KJV: 5:2 But how can I receiue the money, seeing, I know him not?
text.DRV: 5:2 But how I shall require this money, I cannot tell, he knoweth not me, and I know not him: what token shall I give him? Yea neither the way which leadeth thither did I ever know.

text.Base: Then Tobit answered his son Tobias, “He gave me his bond and I gave him my bond. I divided his into two parts; I took one and I put the other with the money. And now look, it is twenty years since I left this money in trust. So now, my son, find yourself a trustworthy man to go with you, and we will pay him wages until you return. But get the money from Gabael.”
text.KJV: 5:3 Then he gaue him the handwriting, and said vnto him, Seeke thee a man which may goe with thee whiles I yet liue, and I will giue him wages, and goe, and receiue the money.
text.DRV: 5:3 Then his father answered him, & said: I have the bill of his hand with me, which when thou shalt shew him he will forthwith restore it.

text.Base: So Tobias went out to look for a man to go with him to Media, someone who was acquainted with the way. He went out and found the angel Raphael standing in front of him; but he did not perceive that he was an angel of God.
text.KJV: 5:4 Therefore when he went to seeke a man, he found Raphael that was an Angell.
text.DRV: 5:4 But go now, and seek thee out some faithful man, that may go with thee, being sure of his hire: that thou mayst receive it, whilst I yet live.

text.Base: Tobias said to him, “Where do you come from, young man?” “From your kindred, the Israelites,” he replied, “and I have come here to work.” Then Tobias said to him, “Do you know the way to Media?”
text.KJV: 5:5 But he knew not; and he said vnto him, Canst thou goe with me to Rages? & knowest thou those places well?
text.DRV: 5:5 Then Tobias going forth, found a goodly young man, standing girded, and as it were ready to walk.

text.Base: “Yes,” he replied, “I have been there many times; I am acquainted with it and know all the roads. I have often gone to Media and stayed with our kinsman Gabael, who lives in Rhages of Media. It is a journey of two days from Ecbatana to Rhages; for Rhages lies in a mountainous area, while Ecbatana is in the middle of the plain.”
text.KJV: 5:6 To whom the Angel said, I will goe with thee, and I know the way well: for I haue lodged with our brother Gabael.
text.DRV: 5:6 And not knowing that it was an Angel of God, he saluted him, and said: From whence have we thee, good young man?

text.Base: Then Tobias said to him, “Wait for me, young man, until I go in and tell my father; for I need you to travel with me, and I will pay you your wages.”
text.KJV: 5:7 Then Tobias said vnto him, Tary for me till I tell my father.
text.DRV: 5:7 But he answered: Of the children of Israel. And Tobias said to him: Knowest thou the way, that leadeth unto the country of the Medes?

text.Base: He replied, “All right, I will wait; but do not take too long.”
text.KJV: 5:8 Then he said vnto him, Goe and tary not; so he went in, and said to his father; Behold, I haue found one, which wil goe with me. Then he said, Call him vnto me, that I may know of what tribe he is, and whether hee be a trustie man to goe with thee.
text.DRV: 5:8 To whom he answered: I know it: and all the ways thereof I have often walked, and I have tarried with Gabelus our brother, who abideth in Rages a city of the Medes, which is situate in the Mount Ecbatanis.

text.Base: So Tobias went in to tell his father Tobit and said to him, “I have just found a man who is one of our own Israelite kindred.” He replied, “Call the man in, my son, so that I may learn about his family and to what tribe he belongs, and whether he is a trustworthy man to go with you.”
text.KJV: 5:9 So he called him, and he came in, and they saluted one another.
text.DRV: 5:9 To whom Tobias said: Stay for me I beseech thee, till I tell these same things to my father.

text.Base: So Tobias went out and called him, and said, “Young man, my father is calling for you.” So he went in, and Tobit greeted him first. He replied, “Joyous greetings to you!” But Tobit retorted, “What joy is left for me any more? I am a man without eyes; I do not see the light of heaven, but I lie in darkness like the dead who no longer see the light. Although still alive, I am among the dead. I hear people but I cannot see them.” But the young man said, “Take courage; the time is near for God to heal you; take courage.” Then Tobit said to him, “My son Tobias wishes to go to Media. Can you accompany him and guide him? I will pay your wages, brother.” He answered, “I can go with him; I know all the roads, for I have often gone to Media and have crossed all its plains, and I am familiar with its mountains and all its paths.”
text.KJV: 5:10 Then Tobit said vnto him, Brother, shew me of what tribe and family thou art.
text.DRV: 5:10 Then Tobias going in told all these things to his father. Where upon his father marveling, requested that he would come in unto him.

text.Base: Then Tobit said to him, “Brother, of what family are you and from what tribe? Tell me, brother.”
text.KJV: 5:11 To whom hee said, Doest thou seeke for a tribe or family, or an hired man to goe with thy sonne? Then Tobit said vnto him, I would know, brother, thy kinred, and name.
text.DRV: 5:11 Going in therefore he saluted him, and said: Joy be to thee always.

text.Base: He replied, “Why do you need to know my tribe?” But Tobit said, “I want to be sure whose son you are and what your name is.”
text.KJV: 5:12 Then he said, I am Azarias, the sonne of Ananias the great, and of thy brethren.
text.DRV: 5:12 And Tobias said: What manner of joy shall be to me, which sit in darkness, and see not the light of heaven?

text.Base: He replied, “I am Azariah, the son of the great Hananiah, one of your relatives.”
text.KJV: 5:13 Then Tobit said, Thou art welcome brother, be not now angry with mee, because I haue enquired to know thy tribe, and thy family, for thou art my brother, of an honest & good stocke: for I know Ananias, and Ionathas sonnes of that great Samaias: as we went together to Ierusalem to worship, and offered the first borne, and the tenths of the fruits, and they were not seduced with the errour of our brethren: my brother, thou art of a good stocke.
text.DRV: 5:13 To whom the young man said: Be of good cheer, it is very near that thou mayst be cured of God.

text.Base: Then Tobit said to him, “Welcome! God save you, brother. Do not feel bitter toward me because I wanted to be sure about your ancestry. It turns out that you are a kinsman, and of good and noble lineage. For I knew Hananiah and Jathan, the two sons of the great Shemaiah, and we used to go together to Jerusalem and worship there and offer the firstlings and the tithes of the produce. They did not go astray in the error of our kindred. You come of good stock, brother; welcome!”
text.KJV: 5:14 But tell me, what wages shall I giue thee? wilt thou a drachme a day? and things necessary as to my owne sonne?
text.DRV: 5:14 Tobias therefore said to him: Canst thou bring my son to Gabelus into Rages a city of the Medes? And when thou shalt return, I will pay thee thy hire.

text.Base: Then he added, “I will pay you a drachma a day as wages, and give you what is necessary for you and my son.
text.KJV: 5:15 Yea moreouer, if ye returne safe, I will adde some thing to the wages.
text.DRV: 5:15 And the Angel said to him: I will conduct him, and bring him to thee again.

text.Base: So go with my son; and I will even add something to your wages.”
text.KJV: 5:16 So they were well pleased. Then said he to Tobias; Prepare thy selfe for the iourney, and God send you a good iourney. And when his sonne had prepared all things for the iourney, his father said; Goe thou with this man, and God which dwelleth in heauen prosper your iourney, & the Angel of God keepe you company. So they went foorth both, and the yong mans dogge with them.
text.DRV: 5:16 To whom Tobias answered: I pray thee tell me, of what house, or what tribe art thou?

text.Base: Raphael replied, “I will go with him; so do not fear. We shall leave in good health and return to you in good health, because the way is safe.” So Tobit said to him, “Blessings be upon you, brother.” Then he called his son and said to him, “Son, prepare what you need for the journey and set out with your brother. May God in heaven bring you safely there and return you in good health to me; and may his angel, my son, accompany you both for your safety.” So he went out to start his journey, and he kissed his father and mother. And Tobit said to him, “Go in health!”
text.KJV: 5:17 But Anna his mother wept, and said to Tobit, Why hast thou sent away our sonne? is hee not the staffe of our hand, in going in and out before vs?
text.DRV: 5:17 To whom Raphael the Angel said: Seekest thou the kindred of an hired servant, or an hired servant himself, that may go with thy son?

text.Base: But his mother began to weep, and said to Tobit, “Why is it that you have sent my child away? Is he not the staff of our hands as he goes in and out before us?
text.KJV: 5:18 Be not greedy (to adde) money to money: but let it bee as refuse in respect of our childe.
text.DRV: 5:18 But lest perhaps I make thee careful, I am Azarias the son of Ananias the great.

text.Base: Do not heap money upon money; but let it be a ransom for our child.
text.KJV: 5:19 For that which the Lord hath giuen vs to liue with, doeth suffice vs.
text.DRV: 5:19 And Tobias answered: Thou art of a great kindred. But I pray thee be not angry that I would know thy kindred.

text.Base: For the life that is given to us by the Lord is enough for us.”
text.KJV: 5:20 Then said Tobit to her, Take no care my sister he shal returne in safety, and thine eyes shall see him.
text.DRV: 5:20 And the Angel said to him: I will lead thy son safe, and bring him to thee again safe.

text.Base: Tobit said to her, “Do not worry; our child will leave in good health and return to us in good health. Your eyes will see him on the day when he returns to you in good health.
text.KJV: 5:21 For the good Angel will keepe him company, and his iourney shall be prosperous, and he shall returne safe.
text.DRV: 5:21 And Tobias answering, said: Well may you walk, & God be in your journey, and his Angel accompany you.

text.Base: Do not worry or fear for them, my sister. For a good angel will accompany him; his journey will be successful, and he will return in good health.”
text.KJV: 5:22 Then she made an end of weeping.
text.DRV: 5:22 Then all things being ready that were to be carried in the way, Tobias bid his father and his mother fare well, & they walked both together.

text.Base: So she stopped weeping. The young man went out and the angel went with him; and the dog came out with him and went along with them. So they both journeyed along, and when the first night overtook them they camped by the Tigris river.
text.KJV: 6:1 And as they went on their iourney, they came in the euening to the riuer Tigris, & they lodged there.
text.DRV: 6:1 And Tobias went forward, and a dog followed him, and he made his first abode by the river of Tigris.

text.Base: Then the young man went down to wash his feet in the Tigris. Suddenly a large fish leaped up from the water and tried to swallow the young man’s foot, and he cried out.
text.KJV: 6:2 And when the yong man went downe to wash himselfe, a fish leaped out of the riuer, and would haue deuoured him.
text.DRV: 6:2 And he went out to wash his feet, and behold an huge fish came forth to devour him.

text.Base: But the angel said to the young man, “Catch the fish and hold it fast!” So the young man seized the fish and hauled it up on the land.
text.KJV: 6:3 Then the Angel said vnto him, Take the fish; and the yong man layd hold of the fish, and drew it to land.
text.DRV: 6:3 Of whom Tobias being afraid, cried out with a loud voice, saying: Sir, he invadeth me.

text.Base: Then the angel said to him, “Cut the fish open and take out its gall, heart, and liver, and keep them with you, but throw away the intestines. For its gall, heart, and liver are useful as medicine.”
text.KJV: 6:4 To whom the Angel said, Open the fish, and take the heart, and the liuer and the gall, and put them vp safely.
text.DRV: 6:4 And the Angel said to him: Take him by the gill, and draw him to thee. Which when he had done, he drew him on the dry land, and he began to struggle before his feet.

text.Base: So the young man cut the fish open and gathered up the gall, heart, and liver. He roasted and ate some of the fish, and kept some salted. The two of them journeyed on together until they were near Media.
text.KJV: 6:5 So the yong man did as the Angel commaunded him, and when they had rosted the fish, they did eate it: then they both went on their way, till they drew neere to Ecbatane.
text.DRV: 6:5 Then said the Angel to him: Take out the entrails of this fish, and his heart, and gall, and liver, keep to thee: for these are necessary & profitable for medicines.

text.Base: Then the young man questioned the angel and said to him, “Azariah, my brother, what medicinal value is there in the fish’s heart and liver, and in the gall?”
text.KJV: 6:6 Then the yong man saide to the Angel; Brother Azarias, to what vse is the heart, and the liuer, and the gall of the fish?
text.DRV: 6:6 Which when he had done, he roasted the flesh thereof, and they took it with them in the way: the rest they salted which might suffice them, till they came to Rages the city of Medes.

text.Base: He replied, “As for the fish’s heart and liver, you must burn them to make a smoke in the presence of a man or a woman afflicted by a demon or evil spirit, and every affliction will flee away and never remain with that person any longer.
text.KJV: 6:7 And he said vnto him, Touching the heart and the liuer, if a deuil, or an euil spirit trouble any, we must make a smoke thereof before the man or the woman, and the party shalbe no more vexed.
text.DRV: 6:7 Then Tobias asked the Angel, & said to him: I beseech thee brother Azarias, tell me what remedies shall these things have, which thou hast bid me keep of the fish?

text.Base: And as for the gall, anoint a person’s eyes where white films have appeared on them; blow upon them, upon the white films, and the eyes will be healed.”
text.KJV: 6:8 As for the gall it is good to anoint a man that hath whitenesse in his eyes, and he shalbe healed.
text.DRV: 6:8 And the Angel answering, said to him: If thou put a little piece of his heart upon coals, the smoke thereof driveth out all kind of devils, either from man or from woman, so that it cometh no more unto them.

text.Base: When he had entered Media and already was approaching Ecbatana,
text.KJV: 6:9 And when they were come neere to Rages;
text.DRV: 6:9 And the gall is available to anoint the eyes, in which there shall be white blemish and they shall be healed.

text.Base: Raphael said to the young man, “Tobias, my brother.” “Here I am,” he answered. Then Raphael said to him, “We must stay this night in the home of Raguel. He is your relative, and he has a daughter named Sarah.
text.KJV: 6:10 The Angel said to the yong man, Brother, to day wee shall lodge with Raguel, who is thy cousin; hee also hath one onely daughter, named Sara, I wil speake for her, that she may be giuen thee for a wife.
text.DRV: 6:10 And Tobias said to him: Where wilt thou that we tarry?

text.Base: He has no male heir and no other daughter except Sarah only, and you, as next of kin to her, have before all other men a hereditary claim on her. Also it is right for you to inherit her father’s possessions.
text.KJV: 6:11 For to thee doth the right of her appertaine, seeing thou onely art of her kinred.
text.DRV: 6:11 And the Angel answering, said: Here is one named Raguel, a near kinsman of thy tribe, and he hath a daughter named Sara, and he hath neither man child, nor any woman child beside her.

text.Base: Moreover, the girl is sensible, brave, and very beautiful, and her father is a good man.”
text.KJV: 6:12 And the maide is faire and wise, now therefore heare me, & I wil speake to her father, and when wee returne from Rages, we will celebrate the mariage: for I know that Raguel cannot marry her to another according to the Law of Moses, but he shalbe guiltie of death, because the right of inheritance doeth rather appertaine to thee, then to any other.
text.DRV: 6:12 All his substance is due to thee, and thou mayst take her to wife.

text.Base: He continued, “You have every right to take her in marriage. So listen to me, brother; tonight I will speak to her father about the girl, so that we may take her to be your bride. When we return from Rhages we will celebrate her marriage. For I know that Raguel can by no means keep her from you or promise her to another man without incurring the penalty of death according to the decree in the book of Moses. He knows that you, rather than any other man, are entitled to marry his daughter. So now listen to me, brother, and tonight we shall speak concerning the girl and arrange her engagement to you. And when we return from Rhages we will take her and bring her back with us to your house.”
text.KJV: 6:13 Then the yong man answered the Angel, I haue heard, brother Azarias, that this maide hath beene giuen to seuen men, who all died in the marriage chamber:
text.DRV: 6:13 Ask her therefore of her father, and he will give her thee to wife.

text.Base: Then Tobias said in answer to Raphael, “Azariah, my brother, I have heard that she already has been given to seven husbands and that they died in the bridal chamber. On the night when they went in to her, they would die. I have heard some people say that a demon kills them.
text.KJV: 6:14 And now I am the onely sonne of my father, and I am afraid, lest if I goe in vnto her, I die, as the other before; for a wicked spirit loueth her, which hurteth no body, but those which come vnto her; wherefore I also feare, lest I die, and bring my fathers and my mothers life (because of me) to the graue with sorrow, for they haue no other sonne to bury them.
text.DRV: 6:14 Then Tobias answered, and said: I hear that she hath been delivered to seven husbands, and they are dead: yea and I have heard, that a devil killed them.

text.Base: It does not harm her, but it kills anyone who desires to approach her. So now, since I am the only son my father has, I am afraid that I may die and bring my father’s and mother’s life down to their grave, grieving for me—and they have no other son to bury them.”
text.KJV: 6:15 Then the Angel said vnto him, Doest thou not remember the precepts, which thy father gaue thee, that thou shouldest marrie a wife of thine owne kindred? wherefore heare me, O my brother, for she shall be giuen thee to wife, and make thou no reckoning of the euil spirit, for this same night shall shee be giuen thee in mariage.
text.DRV: 6:15 I am afraid therefore, lest these things may happen to me also: & whereas I am the only child of my parents, I may bring down their old age with sorrow unto hell.

text.Base: But the angel said to him, “Do you not remember the commandments of your father, that you should take a wife from your own people? Now listen to me, brother, for she will become your wife. Do not worry about the demon, for this very night she will be given to you in marriage.
text.KJV: 6:16 And when thou shalt come into the mariage chamber, thou shalt take the ashes of perfume, and shalt lay vpon them, some of the heart, and liuer of the fish, and shalt make a smoke with it.
text.DRV: 6:16 Then the Angel Raphael said to him: Hear me, and I will shew thee who they are, on whom the devil can prevail.

text.Base: When you enter the bridal chamber, take some of the fish’s liver and heart, and put them on the embers of the incense. An odor will be given off;
text.KJV: 6:17 And the deuill shall smell it, and flee away, and neuer come againe any more: but when thou shalt come to her, rise vp both of you, and pray to God, which is mercifull, who will haue pity on you, and saue you: feare not, for shee is appointed vnto thee from the beginning; and thou shalt preserue her, and shee shall goe with thee. Moreouer I suppose that shee shall beare thee children. Now when Tobias had heard these things, he loued her, and his heart was effectually ioyned to her.
text.DRV: 6:17 For they that so receive matrimony, that they exclude God from themselves, & from their mind, & so give themselves to their lust, as horse and mule, which have not understanding, over them the devil hath power.

text.Base: the demon will smell it and flee, and will never be seen near her any more. Now when you are about to go to bed with her, both of you must first stand up and pray, imploring the Lord of heaven that mercy and safety may be granted to you. Do not be afraid, for she was set apart for you before the world was made. You will save her, and she will go with you. I suppose that you will have children by her, and they will be as brothers to you. So do not worry.” When Tobias heard the words of Raphael and learned that she was his kinswoman, of the lineage of his ancestor’s house, he loved her very much, and his heart was bound to her.
text.DRV: 6:18 But thou when thou shalt take her, entering into the chamber, for three days be continent from her, and thou shalt give thyself to nothing else but to prayers with her.

text.Base: When they reached Ecbatana and arrived at the house of Raguel, Sarah met them and greeted them. They returned her greeting, and she brought them into the house.
text.KJV: 7:1 And when they were come to Ecbatane, they came to the house of Raguel; and Sara met them: and after that they had saluted one another, shee brought them into the house.
text.DRV: 7:1 And they went to Raguel, and Raguel received them with joy.

text.Base: Then Raguel said to his wife Edna, “How much the young man resembles my kinsman Tobit!”
text.KJV: 7:2 Then sayd Raguel to Edna his wife, How like is this yong man to Tobit my cousin?
text.DRV: 7:2 And Raguel beholding Tobias, said to Anna his wife: How like is this young man to my sister's son!

text.Base: Then Raguel asked them, “Where do you come from, brothers?” They answered him, “We belong to the descendants of Naphtali who are exiles in Nineveh.”
text.KJV: 7:3 And Raguel asked them, From whence are you, brethren? To whom they said, We are of the sonnes of Nephthali, which are captiues in Nineue.
text.DRV: 7:3 And when he had spoken these words, he said: Whence are ye young men our brethren?

text.Base: He said to them, “Do you know our kinsman Tobit?” And they replied, “Yes, we know him.” Then he asked them, “Is he in good health?”
text.KJV: 7:4 Then hee said to them, Doe yee know Tobit our kinseman? And they said, We know him. Then said hee, Is he in good health?
text.DRV: 7:4 But they said: We are the tribe of Nephthali, of the captivity of Ninive.

text.Base: They replied, “He is alive and in good health.” And Tobias added, “He is my father!”
text.KJV: 7:5 And they said, Hee is both aliue, and in good health: And Tobias sayd, He is my father.
text.DRV: 7:5 And Raguel said to them: Know you Tobias my brother? Who said: We know him.

text.Base: At that Raguel sprang up and kissed him and wept.
text.KJV: 7:6 Then Raguel leaped vp, and kissed him, and wept,
text.DRV: 7:6 And when he spake much good of him, the Angel said to Raguel: Tobias, of whom thou askest is this man's father.

text.Base: He spoke to him and said, “Blessings upon you, my child, son of a good and noble father! What a terrible misfortune that a man so righteous and charitable should have become blind!” He threw his arms around his kinsman Tobias and wept.
text.KJV: 7:7 And blessed him; and said vnto him, Thou art the sonne of an honest and good man: but when he had heard that Tobit was blinde, he was sorowfull, and wept.
text.DRV: 7:7 And Raguel put forth himself, and with tears kissed him, and weeping upon his neck, said: Blessing have thou my son, because thou art the son of a good and most virtuous man.

text.Base: His wife Edna also wept for him, and their daughter Sarah likewise wept.
text.KJV: 7:8 And likewise Edna his wife, and Sara his daughter wept. Moreouer, they entertained them cheerefully, and after that they had killed a ramme of the flocke, they set store of meat on the table. Then said Tobias to Raphael, Brother Azarias, speak of those things, of which thou diddest talke in the way, and let this businesse be dispatched.
text.DRV: 7:8 And Anna his wife, and Sara their daughter wept.

text.Base: Then Raguel slaughtered a ram from the flock and received them very warmly. When they had bathed and washed themselves and had reclined to eat, Tobias said to Raphael, “Azariah, my brother, ask Raguel to give me my kinswoman Sarah.”
text.KJV: 7:9 So he communicated the matter with Raguel, and Raguel said to Tobias, Eate and drink, and make merry:
text.DRV: 7:9 And after they had talked, Raguel commanded a wether to be killed, and a banquet to be prepared, And when he desired them to sit down to dinner,

text.Base: But Raguel overheard it and said to the young man, “Eat and drink and be merry tonight. For no one except you, brother, has the right to marry my daughter Sarah. Moreover, I do not have the right to give her to any other man than you, because you are my nearest relative.
text.KJV: 7:10 For it is meet that thou shouldest marry my daughter: neuerthelesse I will declare vnto thee the trueth.
text.DRV: 7:10 Tobias said: I will not eat nor drink here this day, unless thou first assure my petition, and promise to give me Sara thy daughter.

text.Base: But let me tell you the truth, my child. I have given her in marriage to seven men, all of them our kinsmen, and all died in the night when they went in to her. But now, my child, eat and drink, and the Lord will act on behalf of you both.” But Tobias said, “I will neither eat nor drink anything until you settle the things that pertain to me.” So Raguel said, “I will do so. She is given to you in accordance with the decree in the book of Moses, and it has been ordained from heaven that she be given to you. Take your kinswoman; from now on you are her brother and she is your sister. She is given to you from today and forever. May the Lord of heaven, my child, guide and prosper you both this night and grant you mercy and peace.”
text.KJV: 7:11 I haue giuen my daughter in mariage to seuen men, who died that night they came in vnto her: neuerthelesse for the present be merry: But Tobias said, I will eate nothing here, till we agree and sweare one to another.
text.DRV: 7:11 Which word Raguel hearing, was sore afraid, knowing what had chanced to those seven husbands, which went in unto her: and he began to fear lest perhaps, it might chance to him also in like manner: and when he doubted, and gave no answer to him demanding,

text.Base: Then Raguel summoned his daughter Sarah. When she came to him he took her by the hand and gave her to Tobias, saying, “Take her to be your wife in accordance with the law and decree written in the book of Moses. Take her and bring her safely to your father. And may the God of heaven prosper your journey with his peace.”
text.KJV: 7:12 Raguel said, Then take her from hencefoorth according to the manner, for thou art her cousin, and she is thine, and the mercifull God giue you good successe in all things.
text.DRV: 7:12 the Angel said to him: Fear not to give her to this man, for to him fearing God is thy daughter due to be his wife: therefore another could not have her.

text.Base: Then he called her mother and told her to bring writing material; and he wrote out a copy of a marriage contract, to the effect that he gave her to him as wife according to the decree of the law of Moses.
text.KJV: 7:13 Then he called his daughter Sara, and she came to her father, and hee tooke her by the hand, and gaue her to be wife to Tobias, saying, Behold, take her after the Law of Moses, and leade her away to thy father: And he blessed them,
text.DRV: 7:13 Then said Raguel: I doubt not but God hath admitted my prayers and tears in his sight.

text.Base: Then they began to eat and drink.
text.KJV: 7:14 And called Edna his wife, & tooke paper, and did write an instrument of couenants, and sealed it.
text.DRV: 7:14 And I believe that therefore he hath made you come to me, that this maid might be joined to her kindred, according to the law of Moyses: and now have no doubt but I will deliver her to thee.

text.Base: Raguel called his wife Edna and said to her, “Sister, prepare the other room, and take her in there.”
text.KJV: 7:15 Then they began to eate.
text.DRV: 7:15 And taking his daughter by the right hand, gave it into the right hand of Tobias, saying: The God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob be with you, and he join you together, and fulfill his blessing in you.

text.Base: So she went and made the bed in that room as he had told her, and began to weep. Then she wiped away her tears and said to her, “Take courage, my daughter; may the Lord of heaven grant you joy in place of your sorrow. Take courage, my daughter.” Then she went out.
text.KJV: 7:16 After Raguel called his wife Edna, and said vnto her, Sister, prepare another chamber, & bring her in thither.
text.DRV: 7:16 And taking paper they made a writing of the marriage.

text.Base: When they had finished eating and drinking they wanted to retire; so they took the young man and brought him into the bridal chamber.
text.KJV: 8:1 And when they had supped, they brought Tobias in vnto her.
text.DRV: 8:1 And after they had supped, they brought in the young man to her.

text.Base: Then Tobias remembered the words of Raphael, and he took the fish’s liver and heart out of the bag where he had them and put them on the embers of the incense.
text.KJV: 8:2 And as he went, he remembred the wordes of Raphael, and tooke the ashes of the perfumes, and put the heart, and the liuer of the fish thereupon, and made a smoke therewith.
text.DRV: 8:2 Tobias therefore remembering the Angel's word, brought forth out of his bag part of the liver, & laid it upon live coals.

text.Base: The odor of the fish so repelled the demon that he fled to the remotest parts of Egypt. But Raphael followed him, and at once bound him there hand and foot.
text.KJV: 8:3 The which smell, when the euill spirit had smelled, hee fled into the outmost parts of Egypt, and the Angel bound him.
text.DRV: 8:3 Then Raphael the Angel took the devil, & bound him in the desert of higher Ægypt.

text.Base: When the parents had gone out and shut the door of the room, Tobias got out of bed and said to Sarah, “Sister, get up; let us pray and implore our Lord that he may grant us mercy and safety.”
text.KJV: 8:4 And after that they were both shut in together, Tobias rose out of the bed and said, Sister, arise, and let vs pray, that God would haue pitie on vs.
text.DRV: 8:4 Then Tobias exhorted the virgin, & said to her: Sara arise, & let us pray to God today, and tomorrow, and the next morrow: because these three nights we are joined to God: & when the third night is past, we will be in our wedlock.

text.Base: So she got up, and they began to pray and implore that they might be kept safe. Tobias began by saying, “Blessed are you, O God of our ancestors, and blessed is your name in all generations forever. Let the heavens and the whole creation bless you forever.”
text.KJV: 8:5 Then began Tobias to say, Blessed art thou, O God of our fathers, and blessed is thy holy and glorious Name for euer, let the heauens blesse thee, and all thy creatures.
text.DRV: 8:5 For we are the children of holy men, and we may not be joined together as gentiles, that know not God.

text.Base: “You made Adam, and for him you made his wife Eve as a helper and support. From the two of them the human race has sprung. You said, ‘It is not good that the man should be alone; let us make a helper for him like himself.’”
text.KJV: 8:6 Thou madest Adam, and gauest him Eue his wife for an helper & stay: of them came mankind: thou hast said, It is not good that man should bee alone, let vs make vnto him an aide like to himselfe.
text.DRV: 8:6 And they rising together, prayed both together that health might be given them.

text.Base: “I now am taking this kinswoman of mine, not because of lust, but with sincerity. Grant that she and I may find mercy and that we may grow old together.”
text.KJV: 8:7 And now, O Lord, I take not this my sister for lust, but vprightly: therefore mercifully ordeine, that wee may become aged together.
text.DRV: 8:7 And Tobias said: Lord God of our fathers, the heavens, and the earth, and the sea, and fountains, and rivers, and all thy creatures that are in them, bless thee.

text.Base: And they both said, “Amen, Amen.”
text.KJV: 8:8 And she said with him, Amen.
text.DRV: 8:8 Thou madest Adam of the slime of the earth, and gavest him Eve an helper.

text.Base: Then they went to sleep for the night. But Raguel got up and called his servants to him, and they went and dug a grave,
text.KJV: 8:9 So they slept both that night, and Raguel arose, and went & made a graue
text.DRV: 8:9 And now Lord thou knowest, that not for fleshly lust do I take my sister to wife, but only for the love of posterity, in the which thy name may be blessed for ever & ever.

text.Base: for he said, “It is possible that he will die and we will become an object of ridicule and derision.”
text.KJV: 8:10 Saying, I feare lest he be dead.
text.DRV: 8:10 Sara also said. Have mercy on us Lord, have mercy upon us, and let us grow old both together in health.

text.Base: When they had finished digging the grave, Raguel went into his house and called his wife,
text.KJV: 8:11 But when Raguel was come into his house,
text.DRV: 8:11 And it came to pass about the cocks' crowing, Raguel bade his servants to be called for, and they went with him together to dig a grave.

text.Base: saying, “Send one of the maids and have her go in to see if he is alive. But if he is dead, let us bury him at once, so that no one will know.”
text.KJV: 8:12 He said vnto his wife Edna, Send one of the maids, and let her see, whether he be aliue: if he be not, that we may bury him, and no man know it.
text.DRV: 8:12 For he said: Lest perhaps it may chance to him, as also to the other seven husbands that went in unto her.

text.Base: So they sent the maid, lit a lamp, and opened the door; and she went in and found them asleep together.
text.KJV: 8:13 So the maid opened the doore and went in, and found them both asleepe,
text.DRV: 8:13 And when they had prepared the pit, Raguel returning to his wife, said to her:

text.Base: Then the maid came out and informed them that he was alive and that nothing was wrong.
text.KJV: 8:14 And came forth, and told them, that he was aliue.
text.DRV: 8:14 Send one of thy handmaids, & let her see if he be dead, that I may bury him before it be day.

text.Base: So they blessed the God of heaven, and Raguel said, “Blessed are you, O God, with every pure blessing; let all your chosen ones bless you. Let them bless you forever.”
text.KJV: 8:15 Then Raguel praised God, and said, O God, thou art worthy to be praised with all pure and holy praise: therefore let thy Saints praise thee with all thy creatures, and let all thine Angels and thine elect praise thee for euer.
text.DRV: 8:15 But she sent one of her handmaids, who going into the chamber, found them safe & sound, sleeping both together.

text.Base: “Blessed are you because you have made me glad. It has not happened as I expected; but you have treated us according to your great mercy.”
text.KJV: 8:16 Thou art to be praised, for thou hast made mee ioyfull, and that is not come to me, which I suspected: but thou hast dealt with vs according to thy great mercie.
text.DRV: 8:16 And returning she brought good tidings: and they blessed our Lord, to wit, Raguel & Anna his wife,

text.Base: “Blessed are you because you had mercy on two only children. Grant them, O Master, mercy and safety, and bring their lives to fulfillment in gladness and mercy.”
text.KJV: 8:17 Thou art to be praised, because thou hast had mercie of two, that were the onely begotten children of their fathers, grant them mercy, O Lord, and finish their life in health, with ioy and mercie.
text.DRV: 8:17 & said: We bless thee Lord God of Israel, because it hath not chanced as we thought.

text.Base: Then he ordered his servants to fill in the grave before daybreak.
text.KJV: 8:18 Then Raguel bade his seruants to fill the graue.
text.DRV: 8:18 For thou hast done thy mercy with us, & hast excluded from us the enemy, that persecuted us.

text.Base: After this he told his wife to bake many loaves of bread; and he went out to the herd and brought two steers and four rams and ordered them to be slaughtered. So they began to make preparations.
text.KJV: 8:19 And hee kept the wedding feast fourteene dayes.
text.DRV: 8:19 And thou hast taken pity upon two the only children. Make them Lord bless thee more fully, & to offer up to thee a sacrifice of thy praise, and of their health, that all nations may know, that thou art God only in all the earth.

text.Base: Then he called Tobias and swore a solemn oath to him, “You shall not leave here for fourteen days, but shall stay here eating and drinking with me; and you shall bring joy to my daughter’s sorrowing spirit.”
text.KJV: 8:20 For before the dayes of the mariage were finished, Raguel had said vnto him by an othe, that he should not depart, till the fourteene dayes of the mariage were expired,
text.DRV: 8:20 And forthwith Raguel commanded his servants, that they should fill up the pit which they had made, before it were day.

text.Base: “Take one-half of all that I own and return in health to your father; the other half will be yours when I and my wife die. Take courage, my child. I am your father and Edna is your mother, and we belong to you as well as to your sister now and forever. Take courage, my child.”
text.KJV: 8:21 And then he should take the halfe of his goods, and goe in safetie to his father, and should haue the rest when I and my wife be dead.
text.DRV: 8:21 And he bade his wife make ready a feast, and prepare all things, that for victuals were necessary to them that go a journey.

text.Base: Then Tobias called Raphael and said to him,
text.KJV: 9:1 Then Tobias called Raphael, and said vnto him,
text.DRV: 9:1 Then Tobias called the Angel to him, whom he thought to be a man, and he said to him: Brother Azarias, I pray thee harken to my words:

text.Base: “Azariah, my brother, take four servants and two camels with you and travel to Rhages. Go to the house of Gabael and give him the bond, get the money, and then bring him with you to the wedding celebration.
text.KJV: 9:2 Brother Azarias, Take with thee a seruant, and two camels, and go to Rages of Media to Gabael, & bring me the money, & bring him to the wedding.
text.DRV: 9:2 If I should deliver myself to be thy servant I shall not deserve thy providence.

text.Base: For you know that my father must be counting the days, and if I delay even one day I will cause him great distress.
text.KJV: 9:3 For Raguel hath sworne that I shall not depart.
text.DRV: 9:3 Howbeit I beseech thee, that thou take unto thee beasts and servants, and go to Gabelus into Rages the city of Medes: and render him his hand writing, and receive of him the money, and desire him to come to my marriage.

text.Base: You see what Raguel has sworn, and I cannot break his oath.”
text.KJV: 9:4 But my father counteth the dayes, and if I tarie long, he will be very sorie.
text.DRV: 9:4 For thyself knowest that my father numbereth the days: and if I slack one day more, his soul is made sorrowful.

text.Base: So Raphael with the four servants and two camels went to Rhages in Media and stayed with Gabael. Raphael gave him the bond and informed him that Tobit’s son Tobias had married and was inviting him to the wedding celebration. So Gabael got up and counted out to him the money bags, with their seals intact; then they layered them on the camels.
text.KJV: 9:5 So Raphael went out and lodged with Gabael, and gaue him the handwriting, who brought forth bags, which were sealed vp, and gaue them to him.
text.DRV: 9:5 And surely thou seest how Raguel hath adjured me, whose adjuring I cannot despise.

text.Base: In the morning they both got up early and went to the wedding celebration. When they entered Raguel’s house, they found Tobias reclining at table. He sprang up and greeted Gabael, and Gabael wept and blessed him, saying, “Good and noble child, son of a good and noble, righteous and charitable man, may the Lord grant the blessing of heaven to you and your wife, and to your wife’s father and mother. Blessed be God, for I see in Tobias the very image of my cousin Tobit.”
